data.description: User will drop new per-face photos into /public/images/banners/ to replace the current campus/events/lab/neighborhood/new-liberal-arts/people/political-club/story JPEGs used as the 8 octahedron face textures in OctahedronHero.tsx (see FRAC-117 for original per-face texture wiring). Filenames + face mapping TBD when user provides files.
data.title: Replace octahedron face textures with new photos

data.body: Shipped 8 WebPs at 1024x1024: story 40.0KB, campus 37.4KB, neighborhood 72.5KB (q22 override - busy content), events 56.2KB, new-liberal-arts 67.8KB, political-club 31.5KB, lab 58.6KB, people 62.9KB. Total 426.9KB (budget 500KB). All faces <80KB. Typecheck + build green; 7 pre-existing test failures unrelated to banners. Branch frac-179-octahedron-face-photos, commit 889c6a9. Note: WebP q45 default, neighborhood needed q22 - flagging in case visual review wants higher quality at cost of going over the 80KB per-face budget.

Review (cold, fresh context, did not write the code).

CODE/BUILD CHECKS — all pass:
- Diff matches plan: scripts/compress-banners.mjs (idempotent, logs sizes, exits non-zero on budget violation, q22 neighborhood override documented in code comment with rationale), OctahedronHero.tsx FACE_BANNER_IMAGES + HouseBanner.tsx BANNER_IMAGES correctly point at all 8 .webp files, internal keys (school/forum/neighborhood) preserved so three.js face indices still align.
- .gitignore: assets-src/ + PNG patterns added correctly.
- package.json: sharp ^0.34.5 added as devDep, build:banners script added, NOT wired into pnpm build (good).
- assets-src/README.md: short, clear.
- All 8 old .jpeg deleted, all 8 new .webp added.
- pnpm typecheck: clean. pnpm build: clean, dist/images/banners/ has 8 .webp, no .jpeg. grep for banners/*.jpeg in src/ and dist/: zero results.
- pnpm test: 141 pass / 7 fail (matches pre-existing failure count, no new failures).

BUDGET: 8 webp total 437,114 bytes = 426.9 KB (under 500 KB). All 8 faces under 80 KB per-face (largest: neighborhood 74.2 KB, smallest: political-club 32.3 KB). Reported sizes match.

VISUAL QUALITY ASSESSMENT:
Examined all 6 highest-risk faces via Read tool. Critical context: every photo has a strong intentional monochrome brand-treatment baked into the source PNG (story=sepia/yellow, campus=green, neighborhood=green halftone, lab=pink, people=sepia, new-liberal-arts=red). These color casts are NOT compression artifacts.

- story.webp (q45, 40 KB): Painterly look, sepia tint preserved. Faces of figures recognizable. Acceptable.
- campus.webp (q45, 38 KB): Green painterly treatment intact. Subjects readable. Acceptable.
- people.webp (q45, 63 KB): Best of the bunch — looks like the original painterly artwork held up well. Good.
- lab.webp (q45, 60 KB): Pink wash with halftone-ish source styling; compression hasn't degraded it noticeably. Acceptable.
- new-liberal-arts.webp (q45, 68 KB): Detailed red-tinted illustration with text/UI elements; legible at this size. Acceptable.
- neighborhood.webp (q22, 74 KB): The halftone/dot pattern is strongest here. Source already has stylized treatment but q22 has added additional dither noise. AT THE OCTAHEDRON-FACE SIZE this reads as texture, not as broken — the subject (musicians on rooftop) is still recognizable. On the full /house/neighborhood banner where it occupies a larger area, the dithering will be more noticeable but is masked by the existing color overlay treatment in HouseBanner.

VERDICT: PASS. The q22 neighborhood override is aggressive but acceptable given (a) the source already has a halftone-style brand treatment that the compression artifacts blend into, (b) on the octahedron face it occupies <100px on screen most of the time, (c) staying under the per-face budget keeps the asset story consistent. If user reports the /house/neighborhood banner looks bad after seeing it live, future work could bump it to q40 (~145 KB) — there's ~73 KB headroom in the total budget.

PRD: Mobile-first goal honored — 426.9 KB total under 500 KB budget aids mobile load. Brand surface preserved at the deliberate stylized treatment baked into source PNGs.

Acceptance criteria 1-6 all met. Moving to done.
